channel.title: Posts on 0x Dev Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on 0x Dev Blog

item.title: The Omarchy (Dual Boot) Experience
item.author: sharat.nair157@gmail.com (Sharat Nair)
item.description:
History

Linux always felt daunting as a teenager since at the surface it looked like something only tech professionals could handle. But this changed quite a bit over the years as more people wanted to transition to an OS that doesn’t throw bloatware at you which obviously made your system slower and offered quite limited possibility to customize how the OS looked and felt like. But I needed to buy a laptop and my YouTube algorithm generously recommended videos about owning an old ThinkPad and using Linux to get the most juice out of the system. So I caved and bought a used ThinkPad T440S for 75 euros. This was the start of the my Linux journey.

item.title: Hello World
item.author: sharat.nair157@gmail.com (Sharat Nair)
item.description:
How I Got Here

I got into the micro-blogging space 2 years ago when I first started my job as a Frontend Platform Engineer. It all began when I noticed my colleague casually browsing Hacker News while sipping his morning coffee—which quickly became my ritual too.

I was impressed by how everyday developers had their own blog websites where they shared knowledge and experience with the world, often with a tinge of humor and sarcasm that made technical content a very nice addition to your daily monotonous task of asking people to rebase their PR when shit doesn’t work.
